title: SEMI F47-0706 (Reapproved 0812)E -- Specification for Semiconductor Processing Equipment Voltage Sag Immunity
authors_or_org: SEMI (Semiconductor Equipment and Materials International)

Current revision F47-0706 (reapproved 0812)E; member price $286, non-member $380.
locator: Standard store listing page: scope, revision, and pricing fields
notes: Directly relevant to L06's subfab-power-quality niche -- this is the specific standard that codifies why RF/plasma-tool power electronics must tolerate facility-level voltage sags, a real engineering constraint vendors design against.

notes: News reporting (not the primary filing document) on the listing-day event -- classified T2. Complements L06-042 (financial/customer detail) with listing-day market reaction and a broader named-customer list (Sineo, SMIC, YMTC in addition to Piotech/AMEC/NAURA/ACM).

Solar/photovoltaic power supplies remain the largest single segment (49.92% of revenue).

title: TSMC Supplier Sustainability Standards, Version 2.0
authors_or_org: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC)
claim_supported: TSMC's Supplier Sustainability Standards (Version 2.0, March 18, 2025) require equipment/process suppliers to ground or otherwise remove static electricity from gases and chemical equipment at risk of explosion/fire, and to provide leak-proof devices/leak sensors for areas/machines using liquid chemicals -- direct buyer-side safety specifications binding on L06 subsystem vendors (gas delivery, chamber hardware) selling into TSMC's supply chain.
locator: Standards document, equipment/chemical-handling safety clauses (quoted via search-index excerpt)
notes: Both the v2.0 and v1.1 PDF URLs returned HTTP 403 on direct WebFetch this session (likely bot protection); the quoted clauses come from search-engine indexing of the document text, not a direct fetch. Verifier should retry the fetch (possibly via a different route) before treating specific clause wording as fully confirmed -- the existence and version/date of the document itself is corroborated by TSMC's own ESG article index.

Investment amount not disclosed in the release.

notes: Directly fetched official Singapore government-agency (EDB) release -- a real, dated government-backed equipment/supply-chain development trigger for Singapore, though the investment amount is not disclosed (unlike the 2015 predecessor joint lab, which was S$150 million per a separate news search) -- flagged so the verifier does not conflate the two.

title: Tata Electronics and Powerchip Semiconductor Manufacturing Corporation (PSMC) Complete Landmark Agreement for Technology Transfer to Build India's First Semiconductor Fab
authors_or_org: Tata Electronics Private Limited (Tata Sons); Powerchip Semiconductor Manufacturing Corporation (PSMC, Taiwan)
claim_supported: On 2024-09-26, Tata Electronics and Taiwan's PSMC signed a technology-transfer and definitive agreement to build India's first greenfield logic fab in Dholera, Gujarat, investment up to INR 91,000 crore (~US$11 billion), capacity up to 50,000 wafer starts/month, targeting power-management ICs, display drivers, microcontrollers, and HPC logic; expected to create 20,000+ direct/indirect jobs. A separate India Semiconductor Mission Fiscal Support Agreement (per PIB and business-press reporting) provides 50% government fiscal support for the project (~INR 91,526 crore total project value per that agreement).
locator: Press release body: date, parties, investment figure, capacity, and product-mix sections
notes: A dated, named, government-linked India fab-construction trigger implying substantial forthcoming demand for etch/deposition/implant/RF/gas-delivery subsystems (to be purchased from AMAT/TEL/Lam/Axcelis-tier suppliers), though the press release itself does not itemize L06 subsystem-level purchases. The companion PIB (Press Information Bureau, Government of India) release confirming the Fiscal Support Agreement returned HTTP 403 on direct fetch this session; its figures (INR 91,526 crore) are cited here via secondary business-press corroboration (Business Upturn, Business Standard) rather than a direct government fetch -- flagged for the verifier to re-attempt pib.gov.in directly.

title: China's Domestic Chip Equipment Adoption Beats 2025 Target at 35%, Led by NAURA, AMEC
authors_or_org: TrendForce
claim_supported: China's domestically manufactured semiconductor equipment share rose from 25% (2024) to 35% (2025), exceeding the 30% target; by category, etching/thin-film deposition >40%, metrology 25%, lithography 18%. NAURA's oxidation/diffusion furnaces exceed 60% share on SMIC's 28nm lines; AMEC's 5nm etch tool entered TSMC advanced-process validation; Piotech's PECVD share at YMTC doubled to 30%; ACM Research cleaning tools hold >90% utilization at Hua Hong's 28nm lines.
locator: Article body: localization percentages and named-company achievements
notes: TrendForce is a Taiwan-headquartered semiconductor research/trade-press firm reporting on Chinese mainland equipment localization -- tagged with both CN and TW geography. Notably conflicts with L06-052's figures (25%->35% here vs. 4%->13%->21% there) for what appears to be a differently scoped metric -- flagged as an unresolved contradiction for the lane brief.

title: China's Semiconductor Equipment Localization Rate Jumps to 21%: Etching Tools Enter TSMC Lines, Lithography Remains the Final Hurdle
authors_or_org: BigGo Finance (Tianxia Gongchang / faxiangongchang.com research network)
claim_supported: China's overall semiconductor equipment localization progressed roughly 4% (2018) -> 13% -> 21% (2025). By category: etching 31%, thin-film deposition ~27%, cleaning equipment >50% in some sub-segments; lithography/coating-developing/metrology-inspection below 10%, described as 'the final and hardest chasm.' AMEC tools deployed on TSMC's 5nm lines; SMEE delivered its first 28nm immersion lithography machine in 2025; NAURA H1 2025 revenue ~$2.2 billion.
locator: Article body: localization-rate trajectory and named-company milestones
notes: Directly conflicts with L06-051's 25%->35% figure for what is nominally the same 'China equipment localization rate' metric -- an unresolved contradiction (likely different scope/methodology between the two research sources) to be flagged explicitly in the lane brief and not averaged.
